CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) SELECT id + 5, name || '_new' FROM test_table WHERE id < 3;
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'UpdatedTest' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'Test%');
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(100), FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT t.name, a.description FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.ref_id WHERE t.id > 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE id > 2;
